description: Implement the global Edit Mode / Preview Mode feature as a foundational application state. This replaces the existing editMode state with a full appMode system that gates ALL editing operations, blocks database writes in Preview Mode, and adds a visible mode toggle in the header.
steps: Step 1: Rename the existing editMode state (line 558 of App.jsx) to appMode with values 'edit' and 'preview'. Update the existing M-key toggle (line 2026-2041) to work with the new state. Create a derived isPreviewMode constant for convenience (const isPreviewMode = appMode === 'preview').

Step 2: Add a visible mode toggle button in the header toolbar (around line 5285 in App.jsx, in the right section near undo/redo buttons). The toggle should show the current mode clearly using the Eye icon (lucide-react) for Preview and Pencil icon for Edit, with distinct colors (e.g., amber/orange for Preview, indigo/blue for Edit). Include mode label text visible on sm+ screens.

Step 3: Gate all auto-save useEffects to not execute when in Preview Mode. In the three debounced save effects (workspace autosave at ~line 1228, task autosave at ~line 1316, metadata autosave at ~line 1340), add an early return if isPreviewMode is true. This ensures no database writes occur during Preview Mode.

Step 4: Gate core editing functions with isPreviewMode check. Add early returns to: addNode (line 4030), deleteNode (line 4524), duplicateNode (line 4360), updateNode (line 4479), createGroup, deleteGroup, addPin, deleteImage, and any other data-modifying functions. Use a guard pattern: if (isPreviewMode) return;

Step 5: Gate drag operations. In handlePointerDownMain (line 3509), prevent initiating node/group/image drags when isPreviewMode. Keep panning allowed. In the node's onPointerDown handler (around line 5994), prevent initiating drags when in preview mode. Same for group and image drag initiation.

Step 6: Gate context menus. Either prevent the context menus from appearing in Preview Mode (set contextMenu/nodeContextMenu/groupContextMenu to null on right-click), or show them but with all editing options disabled/hidden. The simpler approach: suppress context menus entirely in Preview Mode by adding isPreviewMode check in handleContextMenu and the node/group context menu handlers.

Step 7: Gate keyboard shortcuts that create/modify data. In the N-key handler (line 2158, adds a card), the Delete key handler, and any other keyboard handlers that modify data, add isPreviewMode checks. Keep navigation shortcuts (zoom, pan, workspace switching) working.

Step 8: Gate inline text editing. Update the onClick handlers for card title (line 6067) and content (line 6089) to check !isPreviewMode before entering edit mode. Also prevent the '+ Add notes...' click from activating. Update the hover toolbar on cards (delete button, color picker, link picker) to be hidden or disabled in Preview Mode.
Step 9: Gate operations in the connection ports. Prevent initiating connections (the crosshair ports on cards) when in Preview Mode by checking isPreviewMode in the port's onPointerDown handler.
Step 10: Gate the mobile sheet actions. In the mobile bottom sheet (around line 6920), disable Duplicate, Delete, Change Theme, and Disconnect buttons when in Preview Mode.
Step 11: Gate the multi-select action bar. When in Preview Mode, hide or disable Delete, Duplicate, Link, and Group actions in the floating selection menu (around line 6970).

Step 12: Gate sidebar editing actions. In the left sidebar, disable/hide the 'Add New Workspace' button, workspace delete/duplicate buttons, and workspace rename (double-click) when in Preview Mode. Keep workspace switching (clicking to navigate) working.

Step 13: Gate the 'More actions' menu items. In the more menu dropdown (around line 5430), disable or hide: Auto-Align Canvas, New Group, Add Card, Import All Data, Partial Import, Clear Canvas. Keep Export All Data working since it is read-only.

Step 14: Implement mode switching behavior. When switching from Edit to Preview: cancel any in-progress editing (clear editingTextNode, clear dragging states, close context menus), trigger a re-render by forcing state refresh. When switching from Preview to Edit: reload data from the server database by calling hydrateProject for the active project and updating the in-memory state with fresh data from Firestore/localStorage.

Step 15: Pass isPreviewMode to child components that have editing capabilities. Pass it as a prop to CardEditorPanel, FullTaskManager, PinPanel, and ReminderPanel. In those components, gate editing operations (add/edit/delete) behind the isPreviewMode prop. For CardEditorPanel: disable editing inputs. For FullTaskManager: disable task creation, editing, deletion, and status changes. For PinPanel: disable pin creation, editing, and deletion. For ReminderPanel: disable reminder creation, editing, and deletion.
Step 16: Prevent pin dragging in Preview Mode. In the pin's onPointerDown handler (around line 6200), add isPreviewMode check to prevent initiating pin drags.
Step 17: Prevent image dragging and resizing in Preview Mode. In image onPointerDown handlers, add isPreviewMode checks.
Step 18: Prevent group resizing in Preview Mode. In the group resize handle's onPointerDown, add isPreviewMode check.

Step 19: Add visual indicator when in Preview Mode. Add a subtle banner or border effect (e.g., a thin colored bar at the top, or change the canvas background slightly) to make it unmistakably clear that the user is in Preview Mode.
Step 20: Verify the build compiles without errors by running npm run build.
acceptance_criteria: A global appMode state exists with 'edit' and 'preview' values
A clearly visible toggle button exists in the header toolbar showing the current mode
The M key toggles between modes (existing shortcut preserved)
No database writes (localStorage or Firestore) occur when in Preview Mode
All node/group/pin/image creation is blocked in Preview Mode
All deletion operations are blocked in Preview Mode
All drag-to-move operations on nodes, groups, pins, and images are blocked in Preview Mode
Group resizing is blocked in Preview Mode
Inline text editing (card title/content) is blocked in Preview Mode
Connection creation (link ports) is blocked in Preview Mode
Context menus with editing options are suppressed in Preview Mode
Keyboard shortcuts that modify data are blocked in Preview Mode
Pan and zoom still work in Preview Mode
Workspace navigation still works in Preview Mode
Opening cards to read content still works in Preview Mode
Search functionality still works in Preview Mode
Switching to Preview Mode cancels any in-progress edits and refreshes UI state
Switching back to Edit Mode reloads latest data from the server/storage
Child components (CardEditorPanel, FullTaskManager, PinPanel, ReminderPanel) respect Preview Mode
npm run build succeeds without errors
verification: Run npm run build and confirm exit code is 0 with no compilation errors
